place: Colton CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Sunday Rock, a 64,000‑pound glacial boulder, sits in Colton and was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 2009.
The Sunday Rock Legacy Project, founded in 2012, researches and celebrates Colton’s history every year.
Colton’s historic center is flanked by the Raquette River, which drops 40 feet through the hamlet before heading north to the St. Lawrence River.
New York State Route 56 cuts through Colton, linking the hamlet to Potsdam 9 miles north and to the Adirondacks 23 miles southeast.
The Colton Historical Society, Colton‑Pierrepont Central School, and Grasse River Players collaborate each year on projects that blend history, theater, and education.
Colton is the only community in the state that has a single rock listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
